supply_chain.dependencies: Principal materials used are metals and plastics. Most significant raw materials available through multiple sources. Purchase raw materials and components on open market and rely on third parties for certain finished goods.
supply_chain.risks: Cost of products sold may be affected by changes in market price of raw materials and tariffs on certain raw materials, particularly imports from China. Disruptions in availability of raw materials, components and sourced finished goods. Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical tensions, conflicts in Europe and Middle East, and COVID-19 pandemic impacts.

regulatory_risks: Data privacy regulations including GDPR and CCPA
Environmental regulations on air and water emissions, waste management and climate change
Corruption and bribery laws including FCPA and UK Bribery Act
Changes in tax laws and regulations
Tariff changes and trade policy modifications
Environmental compliance costs and remediation obligations
Strict liability for hazardous substance contamination
Permit and license requirements for manufacturing operations

sanction_risks: Trade Laws may impact ability to transact business involving certain countries, items or counterparties
Conflicts in Europe and Middle East creating increased volatility
US-China trade tensions and tariff impacts
Proposed significant tariff increases on foreign imports from Canada, China and Mexico by incoming US administration
Political instability and threats of war or terrorism
Brexit-related currency volatility and regulatory changes
